subject: Ecology
title: Soil Texture and Structure
description: A 60-second lesson on Soil Texture and Structure.

hook.text: Sand, silt, and clay—the ratio of these three particles defines every soil's personality.

buildup.text: Sandy soils drain fast but hold few nutrients. Clay soils hold water but suffocate roots.

discovery.text: Loam—a balanced mix—is ideal for farming because it holds both water and air.

twist.text: Structure matters too. Aggregates clump soil into crumbs that let roots and water move freely.

climax.text: Fungi and organic matter glue aggregates together. Compaction destroys them.

punchline.text: Good soil is a mix of particles and air.

quiz.question: Why is loam considered ideal for farming?
options: It holds both water and air in balance
It is the deepest soil type
